commands.review_summary:
This draft PR extracts shared Ed25519 PEM/raw helpers from device identity and adds an isolated signed-envelope verifier plus tests for hosted official external plugin catalog feeds.

PR surface: Source +212, Tests +192. Total +404 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature-foundation PR, not a bug report. The relevant checks are source inspection and the PR's terminal harness proof for signed and tampered envelopes.

target.title: feat: add hosted feed envelope verifier
target.body:
Related: https://github.com/openclaw/rfcs/pull/25

## What Problem This Solves

Starts the hosted feed trust-verification implementation series by adding the signed envelope verifier primitives that later feed refresh and source-profile PRs can consume.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds a small, isolated verifier for OpenClaw-hosted external plugin catalog envelopes. It verifies Ed25519 signatures over stable base64url payload bytes, accepts PEM or raw base64url public keys, validates the payload type, and only parses the feed after a trusted signature verifies.

This PR intentionally does not wire signed envelopes into marketplace refresh, config, snapshot persistence, CLI output, or install eligibility. Those stay in follow-up PRs from the RFC trust series.

The Ed25519 PEM/raw-key verification helpers are shared with device identity code so the new feed verifier does not fork a second signature implementation.

## User Impact

No user-visible behavior changes yet. This is a draft implementation foundation for future signed hosted feed verification.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
Signed hosted feed envelopes can be verified against an explicit trusted Ed25519 key, and payload bytes changed after signing are rejected before the feed is trusted.

Real environment tested:
Windows checkout `C:\src\openclaw-feeds-trust-envelope` at PR head `8742cc6515`; source harness using `node --import tsx -`; generated in-memory Ed25519 proof key; no network service or persisted cache involved.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Run a source harness that imports `createOfficialExternalPluginCatalogEnvelopePayload`, `createOfficialExternalPluginCatalogEnvelopeSigningInput`, and `verifyOfficialExternalPluginCatalogSignedEnvelope` from `src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog-envelope.ts`.
2. Generate an Ed25519 keypair, sign a minimal `clawhub-official-proof` feed envelope, verify it with the trusted public key, then change the payload sequence and verify the original signature no longer validates.

Evidence after fix:
```text
> node --import tsx -
PROOF:accepted=true
PROOF:signedBy=clawhub-root-proof
PROOF:feedId=clawhub-official-proof
PROOF:entryCount=1
PROOF:tamperedOk=false
PROOF:tamperedError=invalid-signature
```

Observed result after fix:
The verifier accepted the signed feed envelope with the trusted key and returned `signedBy=clawhub-root-proof`; the same signature rejected the tampered payload with `invalid-signature`.

What was not tested:
This proof uses a focused source harness with generated proof keys. It does not test live ClawHub hosted envelope publication, marketplace refresh wiring, config trust-root selection, snapshot trust-state persistence, CLI output, or install eligibility because those are intentionally deferred to later PRs in the trust series.

## Validation

```text
> node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/device-identity.test.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog-envelope.test.ts
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  15 passed (15)

> pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/infra/ed25519-signature.ts src/infra/device-identity.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog-envelope.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog-envelope.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

> pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/infra/ed25519-signature.ts src/infra/device-identity.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog-envelope.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog-envelope.test.ts
PASS

> git diff --cached --check
PASS
```
